messages.author: C2D
messages.text: pretty slick map. how does it work passively?
messages.content_html: pretty slick map. how does it work passively?

messages.author: DO1XX - Dirk
messages.text: Thanks! It reads MeshCore packets, checks the last hop in the route, and combines the repeater ID + RSSI + mapper’s GPS position. All passive - just run the app and every received packet adds a data point. We have quite a lot of traffic here already, so I’m planning to also integrate the Discovery function soon for even more coverage data.
messages.content_html: Thanks! It reads MeshCore packets, checks the last hop in the route, and combines the repeater ID + RSSI + mapper’s GPS position. All passive - just run the app and every received packet adds a data point. We have quite a lot of traffic here already, so I’m planning to also integrate the Discovery function soon for even more coverage data.

messages.author: DO1XX - Dirk
messages.text: How it works: Your phone connects via Bluetooth to a MeshCore Companion device . The Companion device constantly receives packets that are traveling through the mesh network - messages, adverts, everything. The mapper app listens to this RX stream and combines two things: 1. Your current GPS position (from your phone) 2. What packets you’re receiving (from the Companion) Every few seconds, there’s mesh traffic - someone sending a message, a repeater broadcasting an advert, etc. Each time you receive something, the app logs: “At this GPS location, I received a signal.” The path tells us which repeater: Every packet that travels through the mesh has a path - the list of repeaters it hopped through. So if you receive a packet with path ['a1', 'b2'], you know: ∙ You have coverage from repeater b2 (the last hop) ∙ The signal traveled: origin → repeater a1 → repeater b2 → you The main purpose: Building a coverage map - showing where you have reception and where you don’t. Green hexes = signal received. Gray hexes = visited but no signal. Over time, as many people map different areas, we get a complete picture of the mesh network’s coverage. No active transmitting needed - you’re just passively listening to mesh traffic and logging where you received it.​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​ If you’re in an area with no mesh activity, there’s nothing to listen to. In that case, you can switch to TX mode - the mapper will actively send packets. If a repeater receives your packet and responds, you’ve confirmed coverage. This is useful for mapping remote areas, but generates traffic, so passive RX mode is preferred when possible.​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​

So if you receive a packet with path ['a1', 'b2'], you know: ∙ You have coverage from repeater b2 (the last hop) ∙ The signal traveled: origin → repeater a1 → repeater b2 → you The main purpose: Building a coverage map - showing where you have reception and where you don’t. Green hexes = signal received. Gray hexes = visited but no signal. Over time, as many people map different areas, we get a complete picture of the mesh network’s coverage. No active transmitting needed - you’re just passively listening to mesh traffic and logging where you received it.​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​ If you’re in an area with no mesh activity, there’s nothing to listen to. In that case, you can switch to TX mode - the mapper will actively send packets. If a repeater receives your packet and responds, you’ve confirmed coverage. This is useful for mapping remote areas, but generates traffic, so passive RX mode is preferred when possible.​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​

messages.author: DO1XX - Dirk
messages.text: MeshMap Connect is here! The new version features adaptive TX (automatically adjusts to network traffic), rainbow RX animation (colored display shows different packet types), and manual ping every 30 seconds. Privacy modes: Ghost (24h delay), Normal (3h delay), Live (real-time). All old mapper.html links should automatically redirect - please test: https://mapme.sh/connect.html

messages.author: HarveyKG
messages.text: Great app! Is it by design that I get all these pings of other nodes in the coverage channel?
messages.content_html: Great app! Is it by design that I get all these pings of other nodes in the coverage channel?

messages.author: DO1XX - Dirk
messages.text: Yes, by design! Your TX becomes someone else's RX - so when you transmit, other mappers nearby can receive it as coverage data. This way everyone's traffic contributes to mapping without needing extra pings. That said, if I find a way to send a message where the repeater responds with RSSI and path info without flooding the channel, I'd prefer to use that instead.
messages.content_html: Yes, by design! Your TX becomes someone else's RX - so when you transmit, other mappers nearby can receive it as coverage data. This way everyone's traffic contributes to mapping without needing extra pings. That said, if I find a way to send a message where the repeater responds with RSSI and path info without flooding the channel, I'd prefer to use that instead.

This way everyone's traffic contributes to mapping without needing extra pings. That said, if I find a way to send a message where the repeater responds with RSSI and path info without flooding the channel, I'd prefer to use that instead.

messages.author: DO1XX - Dirk
messages.text: MeshMap v3.5 - Coverage Map Update! The Coverage Map got a major update: https://mapme.sh What's new? Discovery instead of Ping Flood TX mapping now uses native MeshCore Discovery function. This means: No more network traffic! Repeaters respond directly without broadcast messages. You can now map with TX without any concerns. Private Channel Each mapper gets their own encrypted channel. Other users can no longer see your mapping data. Better Display Now shows which repeaters responded with what RSSI: 86(-63dB), ac(-85dB), e1(-102dB) Manual Ping Cooldown Manual ping now has a 2 minute cooldown to prevent spam. Join: https://mapme.sh/connect.html Open in browser and connect via Bluetooth to your Companion. iPhone: Bluefy or WebBLE Browser Android: Chrome Privacy Modes: Ghost - 24h delayed Normal - 3h delayed Live - Instantly visible Not seeing v3.5? Clear your browser cache and reload the page! Feedback welcome!
messages.content_html: MeshMap v3.5 - Coverage Map Update! The Coverage Map got a major update: https://mapme.sh What's new? Discovery instead of Ping Flood TX mapping now uses native MeshCore Discovery function. This means: No more network traffic! Repeaters respond directly without broadcast messages. You can now map with TX without any concerns. Private Channel Each mapper gets their own encrypted channel. Other users can no longer see your mapping data. Better Display Now shows which repeaters responded with what RSSI: 86(-63dB), ac(-85dB), e1(-102dB) Manual Ping Cooldown Manual ping now has a 2 minute cooldown to prevent spam. Join: https://mapme.sh/connect.html Open in browser and connect via Bluetooth to your Companion. iPhone: Bluefy or WebBLE Browser Android: Chrome Privacy Modes: Ghost - 24h delayed Normal - 3h delayed Live - Instantly visible Not seeing v3.5? Clear your browser cache and reload the page! Feedback welcome!

messages.author: nightcrawler
messages.text: Things are working great! I have a few minor feedback items. Could the manual ping button be styled so that it is a certain color when I can send a ping and a progress bar on the button to show how much longer I have to wait to send a ping? Right now I have to click the button to know how much longer I have to wait. It would be nice to have a visual indicator. The second item could just be me not understanding the color coding. The hexes on the map weren't updating when the discover repeaters found new repeaters. Also, sometimes new packets wouldn't register as a change on the hexes either. Thank you so much for a great coverage app!
messages.content_html: Things are working great! I have a few minor feedback items. Could the manual ping button be styled so that it is a certain color when I can send a ping and a progress bar on the button to show how much longer I have to wait to send a ping? Right now I have to click the button to know how much longer I have to wait. It would be nice to have a visual indicator. The second item could just be me not understanding the color coding. The hexes on the map weren't updating when the discover repeaters found new repeaters. Also, sometimes new packets wouldn't register as a change on the hexes either. Thank you so much for a great coverage app!

messages.author: DO1XX - Dirk
messages.text: When you have been in a hex and had reception, this is recorded. On the map at mapme.sh, you will see this hex turn green about 5 minutes later, showing that there was reception, along with the average RSSI value. This was a bug yesterday, but it should be fixed now and work correctly today.
messages.content_html: When you have been in a hex and had reception, this is recorded. On the map at mapme.sh, you will see this hex turn green about 5 minutes later, showing that there was reception, along with the average RSSI value. This was a bug yesterday, but it should be fixed now and work correctly today.

messages.author: DO1XX - Dirk
messages.text: Repeater Coverage Update Repeater coverage maps are back! Click on any repeater to see: Mapped - Real coverage from actual connections Theory - Simpe Theoretical RF propagation model (beta) The theory model calculates coverage based on terrain elevation, antenna height, LoRa link budget and line-of-sight analysis. It's computed on-demand for each repeater. Antenna height is estimated at 10m by default. If you want a more accurate simulation for your repeater, let me know your actual antenna height and I'll update it when I have time. Pretty cool to compare what should work in theory vs what actually works in practice! Currently being used in EU and USA.
messages.content_html: Repeater Coverage Update Repeater coverage maps are back! Click on any repeater to see: Mapped - Real coverage from actual connections Theory - Simpe Theoretical RF propagation model (beta) The theory model calculates coverage based on terrain elevation, antenna height, LoRa link budget and line-of-sight analysis. It's computed on-demand for each repeater. Antenna height is estimated at 10m by default. If you want a more accurate simulation for your repeater, let me know your actual antenna height and I'll update it when I have time. Pretty cool to compare what should work in theory vs what actually works in practice! Currently being used in EU and USA.

messages.author: DO1XX - Dirk
messages.text: MeshMap Connect v3.6.0 DISCOVER_REPLY bugfix Battery display from companion device Cooldown timer for manual ping (ring animation) Dead Zones: Auto-ping starts after 5 minutes without RX (don't kill me, stops immediately on any RX packet ) Help modal added
messages.content_html:
MeshMap Connect v3.6.0
  • DISCOVER_REPLY bugfix
  • Battery display from companion device
  • Cooldown timer for manual ping (ring animation)
  • Dead Zones: Auto-ping starts after 5 minutes without RX (don't kill me, stops immediately on any RX packet )
  • Help modal added

messages.author: DO1XX - Dirk
messages.text: If a repeater doesn’t receive any mapper data for 7 days, it becomes invisible again and will only reappear on the map once it’s received data again. I’m not entirely sure yet whether 7 days is the right value, or if 30 days would make more sense.
messages.content_html: If a repeater doesn’t receive any mapper data for 7 days, it becomes invisible again and will only reappear on the map once it’s received data again. I’m not entirely sure yet whether 7 days is the right value, or if 30 days would make more sense.

messages.author: Lieven B
messages.text: Is mapme.sh using OpenStreetMap map data? If so, I think it is missing attribution. https://www.openstreetmap.org/copyright/attribution-guide/
messages.content_html: Is mapme.sh using OpenStreetMap map data? If so, I think it is missing attribution. https://www.openstreetmap.org/copyright/attribution-guide/
embeds_html:
Copyright and License
OpenStreetMap is a map of the world, created by people like you and free to use under an open license.

messages.author: DO1XX - Dirk
messages.text: thanks for pointing this out! You're right — the attribution was incomplete. I've updated the map to properly credit OpenStreetMap contributors with a link to the copyright page, both in the map attribution control and in the welcome dialog. Should be live now. (edited)
messages.content_html: thanks for pointing this out! You're right — the attribution was incomplete. I've updated the map to properly credit OpenStreetMap contributors with a link to the copyright page, both in the map attribution control and in the welcome dialog. Should be live now. (edited)
messages.reactions_html: 1
messages.reply_html:
Lieven B
Is mapme.sh using OpenStreetMap map data? If so, I think it is missing attribution. https://www.openstreetmap.org/copyright/attribution-guide/

messages.author: Dominiek [RRY36]
messages.text: This bug is back. ! TThere was a small bug where repeaters weren't showing on the map even though they had received mapper data. The "active" flag wasn't being updated properly after registration. This is now fixed - 5,323 previously hidden repeaters are now visible on the map. here was a small bug where repeaters weren't showing on the map even though they had received mapper data. The "active" flag wasn't being updated properly after registration. This is now fixed - 5,323 previously hidden repeaters are now visible on the map.

messages.author: DO1XX - Dirk
messages.text: hey everyone, the native android app for mapme.sh is ready! proper background mapping, stable BLE connection, no more web bluetooth. you know the drill google play and f-droid are pending review, but you can grab the APK now: https://github.com/dirkforpresident/mesh-mapme-android source code is public, MIT license. feedback and bug reports welcome. (edited)
messages.content_html: hey everyone, the native android app for mapme.sh is ready! proper background mapping, stable BLE connection, no more web bluetooth. you know the drill google play and f-droid are pending review, but you can grab the APK now: https://github.com/dirkforpresident/mesh-mapme-android source code is public, MIT license. feedback and bug reports welcome. (edited)
embeds_html:
GitHub - dirkforpresident/mesh-mapme-android: MeshCore Coverage Map...
MeshCore Coverage Mapper Android App. Contribute to dirkforpresident/mesh-mapme-android development by creating an account on GitHub.
